title: Embed the built web UI into the binary with a disk-override fallback
action: In src/web/mod.rs, add a `#[derive(rust_embed::RustEmbed, Clone)] #[folder = "static/"] struct Assets;` and construct `axum_embed::ServeEmbed::<Assets>::new()` configured so unmatched paths fall back to index.html with a 200 (SPA routing) — check the installed axum-embed version's actual API (its docs.rs prose was inconclusive on the exact fallback field name; read the vendored crate source or its docs.rs page for the exact version pulled) rather than guessing. Change `router()` so it prefers the existing ServeDir/ServeFile-from-disk behavior only when `static_dir.join("index.html")` exists on disk (this is what makes config.static_dir a working override per D b300856d, e.g. for local dev after bundling the web UI), and falls back to the embedded ServeEmbed service otherwise (e.g. a single prebuilt binary with no static/ directory next to it). Update src/doctor.rs's web-UI check (~line 165-179) so it no longer reports a hard failure when static_dir has no built UI on disk — it should report the embedded UI as the working fallback (still fail only if truly nothing is available, which should be unreachable once embedding is compile-time-guaranteed, but codify what the check actually verifies now).

truths: With a real static/index.html on disk, requests are served from disk exactly as before (no regression)
With no static_dir present at runtime, GET / still returns the embedded index.html (200), and an unmatched client-route path (SPA routing) also returns 200 with index.html, not a 404
herdctl doctor does not report a fatal web-UI failure when only the embedded UI is present

artifacts.substantive: Assets RustEmbed struct + ServeEmbed wiring + disk-override-or-embedded router() logic, not a stub

key_links: router() branches on static_dir.join("index.html").exists() before choosing ServeDir vs ServeEmbed
prohibitions: No change to the API routes in api_routes()

verify: (cd web && npm run bundle) && cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings

trace.outcome: Embedded the web UI into the binary via rust-embed Assets + axum_embed::ServeEmbed; router() serves an on-disk build under static_dir when index.html is present (config override / dev) and falls back to the embedded UI (SPA fallback -> index.html, 200) otherwise. doctor web-UI check now reports the embedded UI as a working fallback instead of a failure.
trace.verify_output: vite bundle built (static/index.html + assets). cargo test: lib 87 passed / 0 failed (incl. 3 new web router tests), all other test binaries 0 failed. cargo clippy -D warnings exit 0, zero warnings.
trace.verify_command: (cd web && npm run bundle) && cargo test --quiet && cargo clippy --quiet -- -D warnings
